    "R1.2": "The artifact contains a material logical inconsistency: it reports r = −0.38 between disagreement and COMET score, then concludes 'when agents disagree more, the final consensus is of higher quality' — the opposite of what a negative correlation implies. 'Make this stronger' likely requires resolving this contradiction, which is a logical-consistency failure, not merely a prose issue.",
    "R1.3": "Load-bearing: the central claimed relationship (high disagreement → high quality) is directly contradicted by the reported statistic (r = −0.38 implies high disagreement → lower COMET). Every downstream recommendation rests on this alignment. If the sign or interpretation is wrong, the entire empirical section fails. Fixing or confirming this is the highest-stakes obligation under any reading of the delegation.",
    "R1.6": "'Make this stronger' routinely includes rhetorical and prose-level improvement — tightening hedges, sharpening the framing of the main claim, and cleaning up the repeated near-identical summary sentences across the chrF/BLEU paragraph and the per-language breakdown paragraph."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Do you want 'stronger' applied primarily to the logical and empirical argument — which requires confronting the apparent sign contradiction (r = −0.38 implies high disagreement predicts lower quality, opposite to the stated conclusion) — or primarily to rhetorical force and prose quality, treating the current interpretation of the statistics as authoritative?"
}

rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains a material logical inconsistency: it reports r = −0.38 between disagreement and COMET score, then concludes 'when agents disagree more, the final consensus is of higher quality' — the opposite of what a negative correlation implies. 'Make this stronger' likely requires resolving this contradiction, which is a logical-consistency failure, not merely a prose issue.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing: the central claimed relationship (high disagreement → high quality) is directly contradicted by the reported statistic (r = −0.38 implies high disagreement → lower COMET). Every downstream recommendation rests on this alignment. If the sign or interpretation is wrong, the entire empirical section fails. Fixing or confirming this is the highest-stakes obligation under any reading of the delegation.
rationale.R1.6: 'Make this stronger' routinely includes rhetorical and prose-level improvement — tightening hedges, sharpening the framing of the main claim, and cleaning up the repeated near-identical summary sentences across the chrF/BLEU paragraph and the per-language breakdown paragraph.

parsed.clarification_question: Do you want 'stronger' applied primarily to the logical and empirical argument — which requires confronting the apparent sign contradiction (r = −0.38 implies high disagreement predicts lower quality, opposite to the stated conclusion) — or primarily to rhetorical force and prose quality, treating the current interpretation of the statistics as authoritative?
